calendar.matters:
NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Mar-03-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff: Maritsa DiMeglio with Counsel Jacob Harker.
Respondent: Rick Fischer in pro per, present.
Tyler Macmillan and Elizabeth Dervy from Eviction Defense Collaborative for Rick Fischer with regard to the Unlawful Detainer matter.

The parties were sworn.

The Court hears testimony, receives evidence and after hearing the evidence presented, the Petitioner's order is Granted as follows: Respondent shall stay at least 50 yards away from Ms. DiMeglio outside of 984 Valencia Street and 2 yards away from the protected person: Maritsa DiMeglio while in the residence as long as both Parties reside there.

Respondent shall not Harass, Attack, Strike, Threaten, Assault (sexually or otherwise), Hit, Follow, Stalk, Destroy Personal Property, Keep under Surveillance, or Block Movements.

Respondent is to have no contact directly by telephone, send messages, mail or e-mail. Respondent shall have peaceful contact only in response to Ms. DiMeglio's contact.

Respondent cannot own, possess, have, buy or try to buy, receive or try to receive, or in any other way get a gun or firearm and must turn in or sell guns or firearms.

Expires March 3, 2013

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Reset To Mar-03-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Abuse/Dependent Adult TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204, Judge: Mary Wiss, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312, Bailiff: Deputy Capozolli.

Jacob Harker, Esq. for Petitioner: Maritsa DiMeglio, also present.

Respondent: Rick Fischer in pro per, present.

The parties were sworn.

Discussions regarding the matter being heard in the afternoon were held. At the conclusion of the discussions, the Court continued the matter to March 3, 2010 at 1:30 pm in Department 204. The TRO was reissued and will expire on March 3, 2010.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Reset To Feb-10-10 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Abuse/Dependent Adult TRO hearing this day.

There being no stipulation by the Respondent to the Commissioner the matter was heard before Judge Mary Wiss.

Dept. 204, Judge: Mary Wiss, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312, Bailiff: Deputy Capozolli.

Jacob Harker, Esq. for Petitioner: Maritsa DiMeglio, also present.

Respondent: Rick Fischer in pro per, also present.

The parties were sworn.

The Court addressed the parties regarding the posture of the case, the pending Unlawful Detainer action, and the irregularities as to the September 2, 2009 order as set forth on the record. The parties agreed to continue the matter to February 10, 2010 pending the Unlawful Detainer action. The Court waived the irregularities as to the September 2, 2009 order and granted a reissuance to February 10, 2010 (order signed).

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Reset To Jan-06-10 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Abuse/Dependent Adult TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204, Judge: John Dearman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542, Bailiff: Deputy Capozolli.

Jacob Harker, Esq. for Petitioner: Maritsa DiMeglio, also present.

Respondent: Rick Fischer in pro per, also present.

The parties presented argument. At the conclusion of the arguments, the Court continued the matter to January 6, 2010 and stated that it would grant a reissuance to January 6, 2010 (order signed) for two reasons: Mr. Harker will have time to effectuate the Unlawful Detainer and Mr. Fischer will have time to find another place to live.

The temp expires: January 6, 2010.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Sep-02-2009 Reset To Nov-04-09 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Abuse/Dependent Adult TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204, Judge: John Dearman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722, Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Jacob Harker, Esq. for Petitioner: Maritsa DiMeglio, also present.

Brendan Conley, Esq. for Respondent: Rick Fischer, also present.

The parties were sworn.

Mr. Harker addressed the Court and stated that he had dismissed the Unlawful Detainer action for the reason(s) set forth on the record. The Court hears testimony/argument, receives evidence and after hearing the evidence presented, the Petitioner's order is Granted as outlined in the Temporary Restraining Order as follows: Respondent shall stay at least 50 yards away from the protected person: Maritsa DiMeglio. At the residence a 2 yard stay away is ordered.

Respondent shall not Harass, Attack, Strike, Threaten, Assault (sexually or otherwise), Hit, Follow, Stalk, Destroy Personal Property, Keep under Surveillance, or Block Movements, Contact (directly or indirectly), Telephone, Send Messages, Mail or e-Mail.

The Court stated that it was not going to grant the move-out order today, that it was denying the move-out order, and that it was continuing the matter to November 4, 2009 at 11:00 a.m. in Department 204.

The Court stated that the Respondent is to leave his rent in an envelope in the Protected Person's mailbox and the Protected Person is to leave the receipt for the rent in the Respondent's mailbox.

Expires: November 4, 2009 at 11:00 a.m.

The order was signed in open court.

In response to counsel, the Court stated that it had hoped that Mr. Fischer would move out within the two months.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Aug-26-2009 Reset To Sep-02-09 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
